frame_analyses.description: The needle is being manipulated into position for the initial stitch.
The needle is being passed through the first mark on the Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: The needle appears to be aligned well with the mark.

frame_analyses.description: The suture is being pulled through and aligned with the second mark.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Even tension applied, slight deviation from the exact mark.

frame_analyses.description: Beginning the first throw, a surgeon's knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: The suture is correctly looped for a double throw.
Hand switch was executed correctly, good tension.

frame_analyses.description: Performing third throw, another hand switch.

frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean cut keeping within the trainer boundaries.

knot_assessments.notes: The surgeon's knot is secure, with appropriate tension.
Single throw completed correctly with a hand switch.
Final single throw completed correctly with a hand switch.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: The drain slit is completely closed with no visible gaps.

technique_summary: The trainee demonstrated good control and technique, with secure knot tying and minimal deviation in suture placement. Hand switching between throws was executed correctly.
improvement_suggestions: Continue to improve precision in aligning the suture with the marks.
Maintain consistent tension when tying knots.
strengths: Good hand coordination during hand switches.
